name: Nero Wolfe's Finnan Haddie (Smoked Haddock)
recipeIngredient: 12 Bread triangles fried in Anchovy butter
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak Finnan haddie in water to cover for 1 hour. Drain and put into a large saucepan, covering with the milk and water.
2. Bring to a boil, then remove from the heat and let stand for 10 to 15 minutes. When cooled, remove the skin and bones, reserving the stock.
3. Melt butter in a heavy-bottomed saucepan. Stir in flour and cook over direct low heat until smooth.
4. Add cream and 1 1/4 cups of the reserved stock; continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is slightly thickened.
5. Season with pepper and nutmeg. When the sauce is thick enough to coat a spoon, remove it from the heat, measure out 1/4 cup, and set aside.
6. Break Finnan haddie into pieces and fold them into the sauce. Simmer gently over low heat for a few minutes until the fish is warmed.
7. Pour the mixture into a shallow casserole; cover with pimiento and sliced hard-cooked eggs and pour the reserved 1/4 cup of sauce over the eggs.
8. Sprinkle the dish with bread crumbs and place under a hot broiler to brown. Serve with the anchovy toast.
